english: full of grace (Milaraspa's Lama); tse ˳di-la (tshe 'di la) drin čě-šós (drin che shos) rań-gi (rang gi) ma yin (ma yin) the greatest benefactress for this life is one's own mother; bód-la (bod la) bka-drín (bka' drin) čé-ba (che ba) lags-so (lags so) this turned out the greatest benefit for Tibet Glr.; ă-ma (a ma) drin-čéń (drin chen) kindest mother! (says a king to a wonder-working female saint) Pth.; drin dráń-pa (drin drang pa) as a vb., to acknowledge a kindness, to feel obliged; as a sbst. thankfulness, gratitude Thgy.; kyód-kyi (khyod kyi) drin rtág-tu (rtag tu) dráń-pas (drang pas) as I shall always feel greatly obliged to you Dzl.; dei (de'i) bka - drín (bka' drin) dráń-čiń (drang ching) full of thankfulness towards him Dzl.; drin rjéd-pa (drin rjed pa) unmindful of obligations; drin gzó-ba (drin gzo ba), drín-du gzó-ba (drin du gzo ba), drín-láń gláń-pa (drin lan glan pa), drín-láń bsáb-pa (drin lan bsab pa), W. *ḍin-zó tań-če* (drin gzo btang ce) to return benefits, to show one's self grateful; drin gzó-żiń (drin gzo zhing) lan byao (lan bya'o) you shall not have done it for nothing Dzl.; drin - lán-du (drin lan du) as a gift made in return, a return-present.
a corrupt form for dril-bu gsil (dril bu gsil), Ld., = gyér-ḱa (gyer kha).
(spelling?) stump, trunk, of a tree or plant, deprived of top and branches Ld. —

gen. dril-bu (dril bu), bell; dril sgróg - pa (dril sgrog pa) to ring the bell; to publish by ringing a bell; dril-lče (dril lce) the tongue of a bell, the clapper; dril-gzúgs (dril gzugs) the body of a bell Cs., Glr.; dril-sgrá (dril sgra) the voice or sound of a bell, peal of bells; dril-ḱań (dril khang) bell-tower, belfry; dril - stégs (dril stegs) the frame of timber, on which bells are suspended.
= grú-bu (gru bu), grú - gu (gru gu), a clue or ball, of wool etc.

num. six, drúg - pa (drug pa), drúg-po (drug po) cf. dgu (dgu); yi-ge (yi ge) drúg-pa (drug pa) or -ma (-ma) the prayer of the six letters, the Ommanipadmehūm, Glr.; drúg-ču (drug cu) sixty; drúg-ču-rtsa-gčíg (drug cu rtsa gcig) (W. *ḍug-ču-re-čig* (drug cu re cig)), or re - gčíg (re gcig), sixty one; drug - brgyá (drug brgya) six hundred; drug - stóń (drug stong) six thousand; drug - ču-skór (drug cu skor) a cycle of sixty years. — drúg-sgra (drug sgra) the so-called article, presenting itself in the following six forms: pa (pa), ba (ba), ma (ma), po (po), bo (bo), mo (mo).

the space near, and esp. before a person or thing, po-bráń - gi druń gáń-na-ba (pho brang gi drung gang na ba) der ˳dúg-nas (der 'dug nas) alighting on the place before the palace Dzl. Ⅳ, 3; gen. with na (na), du (du), nas (nas). 1. adv. near to, near by, to or at the side of, before, to, off from; drúń-du rtóg - pa (drung du rtog pa) to examine personally, face to face, orally Dzl.; drúń-du ˳gró-ba (drung du 'gro ba) to go near or up to. 2. postp. c. genit. (less corr. c. accus.), śiń-gi drúń-na (shing gi drung na) near, or under the tree, drúń-du (drung du) id.; to or towards the tree; drúń-nas (drung nas) away from (the tree); rgyál-poi drúń-du (rgyal po'i drung du) to the king, before, in presence of (coram) the king; drúń - pa (drung pa), resp. sku-drúń - pa (sku drung pa), one standing near, a waiting man, a page in ordinary Cs. — drúń-˳kór (drung 'khor) train, retinue. — drúń-gnas-pa (drung gnas pa) companion, associate. — drúń-yig(-pa) (drung yig (-pa)) secretary. — drúń-tso-ba (drung 'tsho ba) private physician, physician in ordinary Cs. When preceded by żabs (zhabs) it becomes a respectful term, e.g. in the direction of a letter, where it stands for our 'to' (lit. 'to the feet of N.N.').
1. prudent, sensible, judicious, wise Mil., in conjunction with gčáń-po (gcang po); so also Pth. gčań - drúń-ldan - pa (gcang drung ldan pa). — 2. sincere, candid C. — 3. diligent?
root, of rare occurrence; druńs (-nas) pyuń (drungs (-nas) phyung) exterminated, destroyed root and branch, Lex.
clarified, clear Cs.; bżes-druńs (bzhes drungs) resp. for čań (chang), beer, Ts.
1. v. ˳drud-pa ('drud pa). — 2. drud-drúd (drud drud) pelican Sch.
to have a strong desire, to long, languish, pine, for, Sch.
Ts., dreu (dre'u) Lex., ḍiu (ḍi'u) Lh., drel (drel) Glr., mule, dré-po (dre pho), pó-dre (pho dre) he-mule, dré-mo (dre mo), mó-dre (mo dre) she-mule.
